steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the whole text. The passage introduces Thomas, then pivots with 'But Thomas is not the only Indigenous writer to blend traditional stories with popular literature' to introduce Cardinal. Both writers do the same thing. The main purpose encompasses both, not just one.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘To discuss two writers who blend traditional Indigenous stories with popular genres’: matches exactly — Thomas blends Haisla stories with fantasy/mystery; Cardinal blends Métis oral tradition with horror — ✓
  • Choice B — ‘To urge younger Indigenous authors to avoid writing in popular genres’: the opposite of what the passage suggests; no such argument is made — ✗
  • Choice C — ‘To argue that traditional Indigenous stories are more memorable than most novels are’: a comparison never made in the text — ✗
  • Choice D — ‘To explain why one Indigenous author has achieved more success than another has’: no comparison of success levels appears in the passage — ✗
That confirms A is correct.
